Home » Collections » Borrow Materials
BORROWING AND RENEWING MATERIALS Your library card or a valid ID must be presented to withdraw materials.
A library card is available to all North Reading residents. To obtain a card, you will need a photo ID, preferably a driver’s license with the correct address. If the address is not current, please provide a lease, utility bill, or another document with the current North Reading address.
You can renew materials by calling 978-664-4942, or you can renew materials online. Please note that if there is no hold placed on an item you have, the item will renew automatically.
Please ask a staff member to add a note to your account to give other people, including family members, permission to pick up your items. You may give another person your library card to withdraw materials.
Items that are damaged or not returned will be billed at the purchase or replacement cost.

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "27 Navigator Road, Londonderry, NH 03053","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/nh-the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire_271_md.png","telephone": "603-669-4820","name": "The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire","url": "","description": "The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire is housed in an art deco terminal and control tower building in Londonderry. The historic building was constructed as a WPA project in 1937 and was moved in 2004 to its present location on the southeast side of the Manchester Airport before being renovated. Hands-on exhibits tell the story of New Hampshire aviation history and include a flight simulator, various historical artifacts, and planes and models."} The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire 27 Navigator Road, Londonderry, NH 03053603-669-4820https://aviationmuseumofnh.org/[Details/Reserve]
The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire is housed in an art deco terminal and control tower building in Londonderry. The historic building was constructed as a WPA project in 1937 and was moved in 2004 to its present location on the southeast side of the Manchester Airport before being renovated. Hands-on exhibits tell the story of New Hampshire aviation history and include a flight simulator, various historical artifacts, and planes and models.
Categories: Family, History, Technology, Sky
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits Pick up a paper pass at the Flint Memorial Library main circulation desk.
Library Pass: Free entry for up to 4 people.
Additional members in party must pay appropriate admission fee; kids 5 and under, FREE!
The Museum is staffed by volunteers of the New Hampshire Aviation Historical Society and all admission fees and donations support exhibits, education programs, operations and maintenance.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "87 Mount Vernon Street, Boston, MA 02108","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-by-foot_50_md.jpg","telephone": "617-367-2345","name": "Boston by Foot ","url": "","description": "Boston By Foot has presented walking tours of Boston since 1976. Dedicated to promoting public awareness and appreciation of Boston&apos;s rich history and architectural heritage, their guided tours and programs are conducted by a dedicated corps of over 200 volunteer tour guides. Check their website for a list of available tours, most of which take 90 minutes."} Boston by Foot 87 Mount Vernon Street, Boston, MA 02108617-367-2345http://www.bostonbyfoot.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston by Foot Boston By Foot has presented walking tours of Boston since 1976. Dedicated to promoting public awareness and appreciation of Boston's rich history and architectural heritage, their guided tours and programs are conducted by a dedicated corps of over 200 volunteer tour guides. Check their website for a list of available tours, most of which take 90 minutes.
Categories: Children, Family, History, Tours
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Each pass provides free admission for up to 6 on Boston By Foot's public walking tours.
Please consult www.bostonbyfoot.org for tour details; pre-registration is required for all tours.
Reserve this pass to receive discount code. Not valid on private tours, water-based tours, or the two annual celebration tours (Beacon Hill with a BOO! & Footloose on the Freedom Trail.)
Currently there is no coupon to pick up for Boston by Foot.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Benefits Admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person.
This is an e-ticket, valid only for one day. You can either print it out and take it with you or display the pass on your phone or device. Either way, please be sure that the pass barcode is visible.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "120 Tyngsboro Road, Westford, MA 01886","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-the-butterfly-place_82_md.jpg","telephone": "978-392-0955","name": "The Butterfly Place","url": "","description": "A winding pathway through an atrium filled with flowers, koi fish, and quail allows visitors to walk among butterflies from all over the world. Staff members are available for questions and &ldquo;show and tell&rdquo; demonstrations. A continuously running video explains the butterfly life cycle, and exhibits enable visitors to observe eggs, caterpillars, cocoons and chrysalides with information about each species. A gift shop is on the premises. There is a public picnic area and free parking is available."} The Butterfly Place 120 Tyngsboro Road, Westford, MA 01886978-392-0955https://www.butterflyplace-ma.com/[Details/Reserve]
The Butterfly Place A winding pathway through an atrium filled with flowers, koi fish, and quail allows visitors to walk among butterflies from all over the world. Staff members are available for questions and “show and tell” demonstrations. A continuously running video explains the butterfly life cycle, and exhibits enable visitors to observe eggs, caterpillars, cocoons and chrysalides with information about each species. A gift shop is on the premises. There is a public picnic area and free parking is available.
Categories: Children, Family, Nature, Science
Pass Benefits Paper pass grants one free admission.
You do not need to purchase tickets ahead of time or make a reservation unless you have 15 or more people and ticket sales end at 3:30 PM.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-discovery-museums_4_md.jpg","telephone": "978-264-4200","name": "Discovery Museum","url": "","description": "Acton&apos;s Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum&apos;s building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. "} Discovery Museum 177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720978-264-4200http://www.discoveryacton.org/[Details/Reserve]
